required_evidence: Action Contract includes assumptions and work-state renders them before write/execute.

mechanism: Ambiguity alternatives instead of silent interpretation
required_evidence: High-risk ambiguity either resolved from source truth or marked ask/block with alternatives.
Permission evidence includes sensitive fields/path/network risk before side effect.

mechanism: Push back when simpler owner-preserving path exists
required_evidence: Final report records rejected over-complex path and chosen DSXU owner path.

mechanism: Stop or ask only for unresolved high-risk confusion
required_evidence: No endless clarification; ordinary ambiguity proceeds with explicit assumption.
Performance request split into latency/throughput/perceived UX/cost/cache metric.

mechanism: Volume and scale question for data tasks
required_evidence: Action Contract captures data volume assumption and verification scope.
Source capsule or schema read binds fields touched; secret/sensitive fields flagged.
Speculative abstraction smell appears in owner review evidence.
New options require user request or owner rationale.
Background/cache additions require explicit workflow owner and tests.
New error branches map to real failure mode or are flagged speculative.
Final report includes complexity delta for high-risk new modules.
New helper must have import/use owner and test obligation.
Provider/tool/permission/MCP/agent additions prove mainline owner.

mechanism: Duplicate behavior merge or replace-delete candidate
required_evidence: Equivalent duplicate becomes merge/delete candidate, not compatibility holding path.
Write/execute target must match allowedFiles or be blocked/refreshed.

mechanism: Changed line trace to user request
required_evidence: Each touched file has whyTouched, owner, risk, verificationBinding.
Diff classifier flags unrelated formatting/refactor changes.
Patch planner records nearby style inference for meaningful code changes.

mechanism: Only cleanup orphans created by current change
required_evidence: Orphan cleanup evidence distinguishes new orphan vs pre-existing dead code.

mechanism: Unrelated dead code mention not delete
required_evidence: Pre-existing dead code is reported as candidate, not auto-deleted.
Writes/executes inside DSXU product core require explicit override evidence.
Multi-file mutation emits impacted entry/test/config/doc risk.
Missing successCriteria blocks or degrades high-risk mutation claim.
Bugfix report includes failure-before and pass-after evidence where feasible.
Refactor requires pre/post test or no-loss baseline evidence.
Plan steps carry verification check and visible next action.
Failure classified, fix attempted, rerun evidence bound.
Final report binds source/test/live/raw/cost/cache as applicable.

mechanism: Simple fast path for trivial tasks
required_evidence: Trivial one-liners do not force heavy ceremony but still preserve evidence.
README/GitHub claims only cite real DSXU evidence.
No DSXU public product feature uses external brand names.

mechanism: MIT notice handled if copying text
required_evidence: If any text/code is reused, license notice is preserved; preferred path is mechanism-only rewrite.
